instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Sum Assured in Life Insurance
A life insurance policy is like a financial safety net that can help you provide for your family’s needs if you pass away untimely. Moreover, with savings-oriented life insurance plans, you can also create a fund for your goals.
When you buy a life insurance plan, you might come across the word ‘sum assured’. Sum assured depicts the coverage of the life insurance plan or the amount that you choose to get insured for. Do you know what it means? Let’s understand.
What is the Meaning of Sum Assured in Life Insurance?
The meaning of sum assured in life insurance plans can be specified as the coverage that you buy. It is the minimum amount that the insurance company promises to pay in the case of a claim. The sum assured also depicts the fixed benefit that the insurance company pays in the case of a claim, irrespective of the actual loss that you or your family suffers.
The sum assured of the life insurance plan influences the premiums that you pay for term plans and savings-oriented plans. In the case of ULIPs, the premium determines the sum assured of the policy. The sum assured in ULIP is usually determined as a multiple of the annual or single premium paid.
How to Calculate the Sum Assured in Life Insurance?
Life insurance plans usually mention a minimum sum assured when buying the policy. You can choose a sum assured that is equal to or more than the minimum amount specified in the policy. In some plans, there might be a maximum sum assured too. This limits the amount of coverage that you can choose.
So, you can choose a sum assured in insurance plans depending on your coverage needs when buying a life insurance policy, provided it is within the minimum and maximum specified limits of the plan selected.
To calculate the sum assured, you can consider your financial obligations and deduct the existing assets that you have. Financial obligations include your existing debts, your family’s lifestyle expenses, and your goals. Adding them up gives you an idea of the corpus you need to fulfil your financial needs. Existing assets mean the savings and investments that you have, including any existing life insurance policy. Adding all your savings and then deducting them from the financial obligations can give you an idea of how much sum assured you would need.
Key Factors to Consider While Selecting the Sum Assured
When choosing the sum assured, it is recommended to assess your needs to find the right amount. The following factors can help you assess your coverage needs -
Age Factor
When you are young, you might have limited financial responsibilities. As such, you can choose a lower sum assured. However, as you age and your responsibilities grow, your sum assured should also be higher to meet increased financial goals.
Income Level
The sum assured also depends on your income. The basic thumb rule to find the right sum assured of your life insurance policy states that the sum assured should be a minimum of 10 times your annual income. So, your income can help you find the right sum assured, and it also determines the affordability of the associated premiums.
Number of Dependents
The number of dependents would also influence the coverage that you would need. If you have higher dependents, you might have higher financial commitments. In such cases, you need a higher sum assured level.
Lifestyle and Financial Commitments
Your lifestyle depicts your basic monthly expenses, and your financial commitments include existing debts and financial goals. Both these factors help in determining the optimal level of sum assured. If you have high lifestyle expenses and existing debts, you would need a higher sum assured. On the other hand, if your debts are low and your goals are also met, you can choose a lower sum assured since your financial obligations are low.
How to Select the Right Sum Assured?
There are different ways in which you can choose the right sum assured for your life insurance policy. Some of these ways are as follows –
Basic method
This is the most basic method, which states that the sum assured should be at least 10-12 times your annual income.
For instance, if your annual income is ₹20 lakhs, you need a sum assured of ₹2 crores or more.
Financial need analysis
This is a holistic method of computing the sum assured wherein you assess your financial needs and find out how much you would need to cover them. You have to consider your existing debts and your expenses and find a sum assured that would be sufficient to cover both.
Human Life Value (HLV) method
The HLV method is a widely used method for sum assured determination. The method estimates the value of a human life in monetary terms based on an individual’s earning capacity. Under this method, the future earnings of the individual are estimated and capitalised using a discounting factor.
For instance, say an individual contributes ₹20,000 every month towards his family’s financial needs. This means an annual contribution of ₹2.4 lakhs. In the individual’s absence, the family would lose this amount. So, the sum assured should be such that, when invested, it can generate an annual income of ₹2.4 lakhs so that the family’s needs are taken care of. If the risk-free interest rate is assumed to be 6% p.a., the sum assured would be –
Sum assured = ₹2.4 lakhs / 6% = ₹40 lakhs
Underwriter’s thumb rule
The underwriters’ thumb rule also estimates the sum assured based on your annual income. However, the multiple used to calculate the sum assured depends on your age, and it reduces as you grow older. It is as follows –
When you are between 20 and 30 years old, the sum assured should be 15 times your annual income.
When you are between 31 and 40 years old, the sum assured should be 14 times your annual income.
When you are between 41 and 45 years old, the sum assured should be 12 times your annual income.
When you are between 46 and 50 years old, the sum assured should be 10 times your annual income.
When you are between 51 and 55 years old, the sum assured should be 8 times your annual income.
When you are 56 years old or older, the sum assured should be 6 times your annual income.
Difference between Sum Assured and Sum Insured
Sum assured and sum insured are two distinct terms and should not be used interchangeably. Here are the ways in which these two terms differ from one another –
Key Takeaways
Sum assured depicts the coverage of a life insurance policy.
You can find the sum assured that you need by adding up your financial obligations and subtracting your existing financial assets.
There are various calculation methods that allow you to find the optimal sum assured for your life insurance policy.
Sum assured and sum insured are two different terms. While the former is used in life insurance plans and depicts a fixed benefit, the latter is used in general insurance and covers the actual loss that you might suffer.
Conclusion
When buying a life insurance policy, assess your coverage needs and choose the right sum assured so that you and your family can enjoy financial security in the face of emergencies. There are life insurance calculators that help you find the sum assured online in a few steps, depending on your financial needs. You can use these calculators and find the right coverage for your policy. If you have an existing plan, review if its coverage is optimal or not. If not, you can buy additional plans to supplement the coverage for enhanced protection.
FAQs
How much sum assured should you choose in a life insurance plan?
The choice of sum assured depends on your goals, existing assets and liabilities, number of dependents, income, etc. You can use online life insurance calculators to find the sum assured that would help meet your goals.
Can you increase the sum assured after purchasing the policy?
Under most plans, the sum assured remains constant throughout the policy tenure. However, some plans come with the life stage benefit, which allows you to increase the sum assured at important milestones like marriage and childbirth.
Why is the sum assured important in a life insurance policy?
The sum assured is important as it represents the claim that you can get from your life insurance policy. So, it is recommended to choose an optimal sum assured depending on your needs.
Does the sum assured affect your premium amount?
Yes, the premium of your life insurance policy is directly proportional to the sum assured. The higher the sum assured, the higher the premium and vice versa.
Is the sum assured the same as the maturity amount?
The sum assured might not be the same as the maturity amount because some life insurance plans can also offer additional benefits like guaranteed additions, bonuses, etc., that enhance the maturity payout. Moreover, in the case of return of premium plans, the premium is refunded on maturity, not the sum assured. Similarly, in the case of ULIPs, you get the fund value on maturity, not the sum assured.
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. output.final_text:
Sum Assured in Life Insurance
A life insurance policy is like a financial safety net that can help you provide for your family’s financial needs if you pass away untimely. Moreover, with savings-oriented life insurance plans, you can also create a fund for your goals.
When you buy a life insurance plan, you might come across the word ‘sum assured’. Sum assured depicts the coverage of the life insurance plan or the amount that you choose to get insured for. Do you know what it means? Let’s understand.
What is the Meaning of Sum Assured in Life Insurance?
The meaning of sum assured in life insurance plans can be specified as the coverage that you buy. It is the minimum amount that the insurance company promises to pay in the case of a claim. The sum assured also depicts the fixed benefit that the insurance company pays in the case of a claim, irrespective of the actual loss that you or your family suffers.
The sum assured of the life insurance plan influences the premiums that you pay for term plans and savings-oriented plans like endowment and money-back plans. Under these plans, the coverage that you choose and your age and other factors affect the premium payable. In the case of ULIPs, the premium determines the sum assured of the policy. The sum assured in ULIP is usually determined as a multiple of the annual or single premium paid.
How to Estimate the Amount of Sum Assured You Require ?
Life insurance plans usually mention a minimum sum assured when buying the policy. You can choose a sum assured that is equal to or more than the minimum amount specified in the plan. In some plans, there might be a maximum sum assured too. This limits the amount of coverage that you can choose.
So, you can choose a sum assured while purchasing a life insurance plan depending on your coverage needs, provided it is within the minimum and maximum specified limits of the plan selected.
To calculate the sum assured, you can consider your financial obligations and deduct the existing assets that you have. Financial obligations include your existing debts, your family’s lifestyle expenses, and your goals. Adding them up gives you an idea of the corpus you need to fulfil your financial needs. Existing assets mean the savings and investments that you have, including any existing life insurance policy. Adding all your savings and then deducting them from the financial obligations can give you an idea of how much sum assured you would need.
Key Factors to Consider While Selecting the Sum Assured
When choosing the sum assured, it is recommended to assess your needs to find the right amount. The following factors can help you assess your coverage needs -
1. Age Factor
When you are young, you might have limited financial responsibilities. As such, you can choose a lower sum assured. However, as you age and your responsibilities grow, your sum assured should also be higher to meet increased financial goals. 2. Income Level
The sum assured also depends on your income. The basic thumb rule to find the right sum assured of your life insurance policy states that the sum assured should be a minimum of 10 times your annual income1. So, your income can help you find the right sum assured, and it also determines the affordability of the associated premiums. 3. Number of Dependents
The number of dependents would also influence the coverage that you would need. If you have higher dependents, you might have higher financial commitments. In such cases, you need a higher sum assured level. 4. Lifestyle and Financial Commitments
Your lifestyle depicts your basic monthly expenses, and your financial commitments include existing debts and financial goals. Both these factors help in determining the optimal level of sum assured. If you have high lifestyle expenses and existing debts, you would need a higher sum assured. On the other hand, if your debts are low and your goals are also met, you can choose a lower sum assured since your financial obligations are low.
How to Select the Right Sum Assured?
There are different ways in which you can choose the right sum assured for your life insurance policy. Some of these ways are as follows2 –
1. Basic method
This is the most basic method, which states that the sum assured should be at least 10-12 times your annual income. For instance, if your annual income is ₹20 lakhs, you need a sum assured of ₹2 crores or more. 2. Financial need analysis
This is a holistic method of computing the sum assured wherein you assess your financial needs and find out how much you would need to cover them. You have to consider your existing debts and your expenses and find a sum assured that would be sufficient to cover both. 3. Human Life Value (HLV) method
The HLV method is a widely used method for sum assured determination. The method estimates the value of a human life in monetary terms based on an individual’s earning capacity. Under this method, the future earnings of the individual are estimated and capitalised using a discounting factor.
For instance, say an individual contributes ₹20,000 every month towards his family’s financial needs. This means an annual contribution of ₹2.4 lakhs. In the individual’s absence, the family would lose this amount. So, the sum assured should be such that, when invested, it can generate an annual income of ₹2.4 lakhs so that the family’s needs are taken care of. If the risk-free interest rate is assumed to be 6% p.a., the sum assured would be – Sum assured = ₹2.4 lakhs / 6% = ₹40 lakhs 4. Underwriter’s thumb rule
The underwriters’ thumb rule also estimates the sum assured based on your annual income and age. The multiple used to calculate the sum assured depends on your age, and it reduces as you grow older. It is as follows –
o When you are between 20 and 30 years old, the sum assured should be 15 times
your annual income. o When you are between 31 and 40 years old, the sum assured should be 14 times
your annual income. o When you are between 41 and 45 years old, the sum assured should be 12 times
your annual income. o When you are between 46 and 50 years old, the sum assured should be 10 times
your annual income. o When you are between 51 and 55 years old, the sum assured should be 8 times your
annual income. o When you are 56 years old or older, the sum assured should be 6 times your annual
income.
Difference between Sum Assured and Sum Insured
Sum assured and sum insured are two distinct terms and should not be used interchangeably. Here are the ways in which these two terms differ from one another –
Sum assured Sum insured The term is used in the context of life insurance plans
The term is used in the context of general insurance plans like health insurance, motor insurance, etc. Sum assured usually pays a fixed amount in the case of a claim. For instance, if the sum assured of a term plan is ₹50 lakhs, the death benefit would be ₹50 lakhs.
The sum insured covers the actual amount of loss suffered. For instance, if the sum insured of a health plan is ₹5 lakhs and your medical bills are for ₹2 lakhs, the claim would be ₹2 lakhs. The sum assured is paid only in the case of the life insured’s demise during the policy tenure or in the case of maturity of the plan.
The sum insured is paid only in the case of a claim. There’s no maturity benefit.
The beneficiary can use the sum assured received from the policy for any financial need.
The claim received can be used only for covering the loss, i.e., for paying medical bills (health insurance), for meeting repair costs (motor insurance), etc. The sum assured usually remains constant throughout the policy.
The sum insured can be changed on policy renewals.
Key Takeaways
1. Sum assured depicts the coverage of a life insurance policy. 2. You can find the sum assured that you need by adding up your financial obligations and
subtracting your existing financial assets. 3. There are various calculation methods that allow you to find the optimal sum assured for
your life insurance policy. 4. Sum assured and sum insured are two different terms. While the former is used in life
insurance plans and usually depicts a fixed benefit, the latter is used in general insurance and covers the actual loss that you might suffer.
Conclusion
When buying a life insurance policy, assess your coverage needs and choose the right sum assured so that you and your family can enjoy financial security in the face of emergencies. There are life insurance calculators that help you find the sum assured online in a few steps, depending on your financial needs. You can use these calculators and find the right coverage for your policy. If you have an existing plan, review if its coverage is optimal or not. If not, you can buy additional plans to supplement the coverage for enhanced financial protection.
FAQs
1. How much sum assured should you choose in a life insurance plan?
The choice of sum assured depends on your goals, existing assets and liabilities, number of dependents, income, etc. You can use online life insurance calculators to find the sum assured that would help meet your goals. 2. Can you increase the sum assured after purchasing a life insurance policy?
Under most life insurance plans, the sum assured remains constant throughout the policy tenure. However, some term plans come with the life stage benefit, which allows you to increase the sum assured at important milestones like marriage and childbirth. 3. Why is the sum assured important in a life insurance policy?
The sum assured is important as it represents the claim that you can get from your life insurance policy. So, it is recommended to choose an optimal sum assured depending on your needs. 4. Does the sum assured affect your premium amount?
Yes, the premium of your life insurance policy is directly proportional to the sum assured. The higher the sum assured, the higher the premium and vice versa. 5. Is the sum assured the same as the maturity amount in life insurance plans?
The sum assured might not be the same as the maturity amount because some life insurance plans can also offer additional benefits like guaranteed* additions, bonuses (if declared), etc., that enhance the maturity payout.. Similarly, in the case of ULIPs, you get the fund value on maturity, not the sum assured.
